title: Investigate likely causes of slash command failure in the current Codex setup on this machine. Focus on repo-local launchers/wrappers vs upstream slash loading behavior. Check local files only by describing what to inspect conceptually; do not edit files. Return hypotheses ranked by likelihood and t...
summary_text: 1. **Very likely: launcher wrapper cannot resolve repo-local `orchestrator-entrypoint-hint` helper** - Why: wrapper scripts in `[~/bin/kernel](/Users/masayuki/bin/kernel)` and related launchers resolve helper paths under `scripts/local/launchers/...`. The file `orchestrator-entrypoint-hint` is not present in the repo tree where these wrappers expect it, so startup can abort before slash command registration reaches the Codex runtime. - Why this is slash-relevant: if launcher startup is broken or exits early, slash-command loading in the Codex session never happens even if the runtime itself is fine. - Most probative evidence to gather: - Confirm exact referenced path in `[~/.bin/kernel]` wrapper and other wrappers (`v`, `vote`, `skill`, `k`, etc.). - Verify whether `orchestrator-entrypoint-hint` exists anywhere in: - `/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/launchers` - `/Users/masayuki/.codex/prompts` - `/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/.codex/prompts` - Check whether missing helper causes nonzero exit before `codex` process exec. 2. **High: installed launchers are stale/out-of-sync with the current repo layout** - Why: multiple launch paths exist (`/Users/masayuki/bin` wrappers, repo launchers under `scripts/local/launchers`), and they hardcode repo-specific absolute paths. If those absolute paths no longer match checked-out file locations/version, behavior diverges from upstream loader expectations. - Most probative evidence to gather: - `which -a kernel v vote skill codex-kernel-guard codex-skill-launch` and `readlink` each target. - Compare installed `~/bin/*` launcher contents against repo equivalents at `scripts/local/launchers/*` and `scripts/local/launchers/codex-orchestrator.zsh`. - Check whether `~/.bin` scripts changed recently vs repo commit baseline. 3. **High: PATH precedence/launcher selection ambiguity (repo-local vs user-local)** - Why: if a different command named `kernel`/`v`/`vote` is found earlier/later in PATH than expected, the active wrapper can differ across shells, causing one wrapper to pass different args/env to the Codex runtime and alter slash loading. - Most probative evidence to gather: - In a fresh shell: `which -a kernel v vote k skill`. - Confirm PATH order from that shell and whether `scripts/local/install-kernel-launchers.sh` points to the expected install dir. 4. **Medium-high: prompt/slash source path mismatch between wrapper and upstream prompt loader** - Why: slash commands are often discovered via prompt files and launch-time prompt root configuration. Repo-local launchers plus `codex-prompt-launch` can override where prompts come from; if root path is wrong or stale, slash command manifests are never found while other commands still run. - Most probative evidence to gather: - Inspect `[scripts/local/launchers/codex-prompt-launch]` / launcher invocation args for prompt path flags. - Inspect `[~/.config/codex-prompt-launcher/orchestrator-shell.zsh]` and `~/.zshrc` for exports that set prompt directories. - Verify slash command definitions actually exist under the effective prompt root (for this checkout), not just globally. 5. **Medium: shell integration not loading orchestration hooks in the execution shell context** - Why: if the current shell session does not source the orchestrator shell hooks, slash completion/registration hooks won’t be injected even if prompts are present. - Most probative evidence to gather: - Confirm sourced files from `~/.zshrc` include the orchestrator hook. - Verify shell session type (login/non-login, interactive/non-interactive) used by wrappers and whether that still sources the hook. - Check for conditional guards like `[[ -r ... ]]` failing. 6. **Medium: guard/wrapper preflight exits early under environment mismatch** - Why: `codex-kernel-guard` and related wrappers may abort when required env/paths are missing, producing a generic failure that appears as command/launcher failure before slash registration. - Most probat...
